title: rubus pallidus subsp. bloxamii
intro: Rubus pallidus subsp. bloxamii, also known as the mountain bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus pallidus plant, a type of thorny shrub native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Rubus pallidus subsp. bloxamii include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, stems, and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
